targets.name: 5-hydroxytryptamine receptor 2B
targets.clinical_consequence: Serotonin-driven fibroblast proliferation → cardiac valvulopathy and fibrosis

targets.name: Potassium voltage-gated channel subfamily KQT member 1
targets.clinical_consequence: IKs inhibition → QT prolongation and increased risk of torsades de pointes

targets.name: Voltage-dependent L-type calcium channel subunit alpha-1C
targets.clinical_consequence: Impaired cardiac contractility → hypotension, heart failure and conduction abnormalities

targets.name: Sodium channel protein type 5 subunit alpha
targets.clinical_consequence: Cardiac conduction block → Brugada-like arrhythmia and sudden cardiac death

targets.name: Beta-2 adrenergic receptor
targets.clinical_consequence: Tachycardia and QT prolongation from off-target beta-2 adrenoceptor stimulation

targets.name: Muscarinic acetylcholine receptor M2
targets.clinical_consequence: Bradycardia and AV block from parasympathetic overdrive via M2 receptor antagonism or agonism

targets.name: Beta-1 adrenergic receptor
targets.clinical_consequence: Tachycardia and myocardial ischemia from unintended beta-1 adrenoceptor stimulation

targets.name: Alpha-1A adrenergic receptor
targets.clinical_consequence: Orthostatic hypotension and reflex tachycardia from alpha-1A adrenoceptor blockade

targets.name: hERG voltage-gated potassium channel
targets.clinical_consequence: QT prolongation → torsades de pointes and ventricular fibrillation

targets.name: cGMP-inhibited 3',5'-cyclic phosphodiesterase 3A
targets.clinical_consequence: Elevated cardiac cAMP → increased contractility, arrhythmia and sudden cardiac death

targets.name: Endothelin-1 receptor
targets.clinical_consequence: Vasoconstriction → hypertension, pulmonary arterial hypertension and cardiac remodelling

targets.name: Solute carrier organic anion transporter family member 1B1
targets.clinical_consequence: OATP1B1 inhibition → impaired hepatic statin uptake and statin-induced myopathy

targets.name: Solute carrier organic anion transporter family member 1B3
targets.clinical_consequence: OATP1B3 inhibition → hepatic drug accumulation and altered clearance of bilirubin and drugs

targets.name: Cytochrome P450 2D6
targets.clinical_consequence: CYP2D6 inhibition → drug-drug interactions with narrow-therapeutic-index substrates such as codeine and tamoxifen

targets.name: Cytochrome P450 1A2
targets.clinical_consequence: CYP1A2 inhibition → elevated plasma levels of substrates including clozapine and theophylline

targets.name: Cytochrome P450 2C9
targets.clinical_consequence: CYP2C9 inhibition → increased exposure to warfarin and NSAIDs, raising bleeding and toxicity risk

targets.name: Cytochrome P450 3A4
targets.clinical_consequence: CYP3A4 inhibition or induction → broad drug-drug interactions affecting the majority of marketed drugs

targets.name: Cytochrome P450 2C19
targets.clinical_consequence: CYP2C19 inhibition → clopidogrel resistance and elevated proton-pump inhibitor exposure

targets.name: Bile salt export pump
targets.clinical_consequence: BSEP inhibition → intrahepatic cholestasis, bile acid accumulation and drug-induced liver injury

targets.name: Muscarinic acetylcholine receptor M1
targets.clinical_consequence: Cognitive impairment, confusion and delirium from central muscarinic M1 receptor blockade

targets.name: D(2) dopamine receptor
targets.clinical_consequence: Extrapyramidal symptoms, tardive dyskinesia and hyperprolactinaemia from D2 receptor blockade

targets.name: 5-hydroxytryptamine receptor 2A
targets.clinical_consequence: Hallucinations, psychosis and serotonin syndrome from 5-HT2A receptor perturbation

targets.name: Histamine H1 receptor
targets.clinical_consequence: Central sedation, cognitive impairment and weight gain from H1 receptor blockade

targets.name: Mu-type opioid receptor
targets.clinical_consequence: Respiratory depression, dependence and opioid-induced neurotoxicity from mu-opioid receptor activation

targets.name: Kappa-type opioid receptor
targets.clinical_consequence: Dysphoria, sedation and hallucinations from kappa-opioid receptor activation

targets.name: Sodium-dependent dopamine transporter
targets.clinical_consequence: Abuse potential, psychomotor agitation and stereotypy from dopamine transporter inhibition

targets.name: Solute carrier family 22 member 2
targets.clinical_consequence: OCT2 inhibition → impaired renal drug clearance and nephrotoxicity risk from substrate accumulation

targets.name: Multidrug and toxin extrusion protein 1
targets.clinical_consequence: MATE1 inhibition → renal drug accumulation and increased metformin plasma levels

targets.name: Prostaglandin G/H synthase 1
targets.clinical_consequence: Gastric mucosal prostaglandin depletion → peptic ulceration and GI bleeding

targets.name: Muscarinic acetylcholine receptor M3
targets.clinical_consequence: Constipation, dry mouth, urinary retention and ileus from M3 muscarinic receptor blockade

targets.name: Mast/stem cell growth factor receptor Kit
targets.clinical_consequence: Myelosuppression and anaemia from off-target inhibition of KIT-dependent haematopoiesis

targets.name: Receptor-type tyrosine-protein kinase FLT3
targets.clinical_consequence: Myelosuppression and QT prolongation from off-target FLT3 kinase inhibition

targets.name: Tumor necrosis factor
targets.clinical_consequence: Broad immunosuppression → increased infection risk and reactivation of latent tuberculosis
